raw:
I'll start by inspecting the changed code and doing real recon on the files mentioned.
Let me look at the core diffs. The `.pi/plans/`, `AGENTS.md`, and `CHARTER.md` changes look like loop side effects — I'll verify those aren't scope violations after the main review.
Now let me read the full reader.ts to understand the context around these changes.
Now the view.ts diff.
Now the index.ts diff.
Let me see more of the index.ts context around the spinner block and how STATUS_KEY/cleanup works.
Let me check STATUS_KEY/DIRECT_STATUS_KEY definitions and all setStatus/setWidget usages.
Let me check where `pushStatus`, `startSpinner`, `stopSpinner` are called throughout the loop, and whether there's a final clear.
Let me look at the broader tool handler structure for error cleanup, and check whether the widget is cleared on errors/teardown.
Let me check how the tool handler wraps this code — is there a try/finally for cleanup, and what happens on exceptions/abort.
Let me find the handler wrapper and any try/finally + the original setStatus cleanup behavior to know if leftover widget is a regression.
Let me check the structure of `execute` for try/catch/finally — whether the spinner timer and widget are cleaned up on exceptions/abort.
Now the test file diff and the spec.
This is a substantial diff. Let me read the full test file, check the docs changes (possible scope creep), and verify what phases activity.json actually writes.
Let me verify what `phase` values `writeActivity` actually writes — this matters for the planner/reviewer role inference.
Let me verify key external facts: the planner/reviewer transcript roles (since the loop only writes phase developer/verify/tester/idle), the initial ledger state, and the pi `setWidget` signature.
Let me verify the initial ledger state and the reviewer gate invocation context.
Now let me check the docs changes (`AGENTS.md`, `CHARTER.md`) since the strict constraints limited blast radius to reader.ts, view.ts, index.ts, and reader_test.sh.
This is a thorough change. Let me verify several correctness concerns. First, the `forceRefresh` / `reloadModel` mechanism and whether `'m'` is picker-scoped or global.
The 'm' key is correctly scoped to picker input only. Let me check the `forceRefresh`/`reloadModel`/polling mechanism and the `currentSlug` selection used after reload.
Let me check `currentSlug()` and the polling loop to ensure the new statusTasks rebuild and snapshot comparison work, plus verify the buildStatuslineModel is unscoped in the dashboard (intentional — shows all sessions' tasks but badges ownership).
Let me check the poll interval and the `jumpToLiveAgent` for completeness.
The dashboard polls every 600ms and now bakes `elapsedMs` (derived from `Date.now()`) into the snapshot. Let me examine performance-sensitive paths: how transcripts are written/sized, since `buildStatuslineModel` now reads the full active transcript on every 120ms spinner tick.
Let me check the transcript caps to assess the read-cost concern, since `buildStatuslineModel` reads the full active transcript on every 120ms tick.
This is a well-structured change. Let me verify the theme color tokens used by the reader are all valid (the reader passes `error`, `warning`, `text`, etc. to the injected `theme.fg`).
Let me verify the theme token names the reader uses are valid by checking the installed pi types and existing usage.
Let me verify the exact `setWidget` signature and `ThemeColor` tokens from the installed pi types.
The `setWidget(key, content: string[] | undefined, options?: { placement })` signature matches exactly. Let me check the ThemeColor tokens and the widget line cap / behavior.
Confirmed: `setWidget(key, content, { placement })` matches, `MAX_WIDGET_LINES = 10`, panel caps at 7. Now let me verify the `ThemeColor` token names the reader passes (`error`, `warning`, `success`, `muted`, `dim`, `text`, `accent`).
This is a large, well-structured change. Let me verify the theme tokens are valid (confirmed: `accent/error/warning/success/muted/dim/text` all exist in `ThemeColor`) and check the timing of the doc-file changes, which are outside the declared blast radius.
Let me verify a couple final correctness details: how the spinner ticker interacts with transcript reads during live runs, and confirm the docs changes are unrelated residue.
Let me check real transcript sizes and how tool_call args are written, to assess the uncapped read on the 120ms ticker.
Transcripts are capped at ~51KB, so the per-tick read is bounded as the task allowed. Let me check how the widget renders lines — whether it truncates to terminal width, which matters because index.ts uses the default width=80 for the panel.
The widget wraps each line in a `Text` component. index.ts calls `formatStatusPanel` without a `width`, so it always uses the 80-col default. Let me check how `Text` handles lines wider/narrower than the terminal.
This is a large change touching many concerns. Let me verify whether the auto-commit would sweep in the unrelated doc changes, and recheck the planner state→glyph interaction.
This is a large, well-structured change. Let me verify a few remaining things: the DASHBOARD-SPEC Phase C contract, and whether the doc-file changes belong to this task or are residue.
The handoffs confirm this task's intended scope is exactly the 4 files (reader.ts, view.ts, index.ts, reader_test.sh). The `AGENTS.md`/`CHARTER.md` changes relate to "Definition of Done"/Gate-2 rationale — a *different* task, uncommitted residue in the tree. Let me verify the gate2→done completion path and spinner cleanup.
Let me verify the protected files are truly untouched and confirm the doc-file residue origin.
This is a substantial, carefully-built change. Most logic traces correctly. But I've spotted a potential serious issue around **staleness gating during long agent runs**. Let me verify whether `activity.json` is refreshed during a single `runAgent` stream, because `buildStatuslineModel` gates all liveness on `activity.updatedAt` being within `staleMs` (20s).
This is a large, careful change. I've found the core logic is sound, but I need to verify a few real concerns. Let me check the auto-commit staging (could the unrelated doc changes get swept in?) and the freshness/liveness interaction.
This is a large, well-built change. Let me pin down exact line numbers for my findings and double-check the staleness/heartbeat behavior, which is central to the "live" claim.
This is a large, carefully-built change. Let me pin down exact line numbers for my findings before concluding.
This is a large, careful change. Let me verify two final things: whether the auto-commit could sweep the unrelated doc changes, and the activity heartbeat cadence (central to the "live" claim).
Let me pin down exact line numbers for my findings.
I have enough to complete the review. Let me confirm two final line citations for accuracy.
This is a large, careful change. I've found a potential correctness issue with the "live" claim. Let me verify how transcript event timestamps and activity freshness interact, since this is central to DELIVERABLE 1.
I have a strong hypothesis about a liveness defect. Let me verify it empirically against real ledger data — whether agent runs exceed the 20s staleness window while `activity.json` stays frozen.
This is the crux. Real developer runs last 8–10 minutes, but I need to confirm `activity.json` is NOT refreshed mid-run (the liveness gate depends on it). Let me verify the dev/tester `runAgent` calls and the freshness interaction precisely.
This is the crux of my main finding. Let me confirm `writeActivity` stamps `updatedAt` with the current time, which couples activity-freshness to elapsed.
I have enough to complete the review. Let me verify the precise line numbers for my key finding and confirm the dev/tester runs have no activity heartbeat.
I have enough to render a verdict. Let me confirm the two exact citations for the blocking finding (the idle-render branch and the stale-test that proves awareness).
I've completed thorough recon. Let me verify one final detail — whether the blocking liveness concern manifests in real run durations vs the 20s staleness gate.
This confirms my primary concern. Real developer/tester runs last **347–628 seconds**, but the liveness gate is **20s** with no `activity.json` heartbeat during a run. Let me confirm whether `pushStatus` fires during planning (to assess a secondary glyph-precedence issue).
I've completed thorough recon. Let me get the two remaining citations precise.
I have completed thorough recon. Let me confirm the exact test-fixture line numbers that mask the staleness path.
I have completed thorough recon across all changed files, the protected files, the pi 0.78.0 API surface, and real ledger data. Let me confirm the final citation for the dashboard live-header path.
## Review complete

I did real recon on all changed files, the protected files, the installed pi 0.78.0 API surface (`setWidget` signature, `MAX_WIDGET_LINES=10`, `ThemeColor` tokens — all verified correct), and real ledger transcript data.

The change is largely high quality: `reader.ts` stays pure and defensive, the `setWidget` wiring/headless no-op guard is correct, session scoping is preserved, protected files (gates/ledger/guard/planner/reviewer/ship/crew) are untouched, and the tests are thorough. But I found one correctness issue that defeats the headline deliverable in production.

### The core problem: the "LIVE" block is dead after 20s of every real run

`buildStatuslineModel` gates ALL live enrichment behind `fresh` (activity.json updated within `staleMs ?? 20000`):

- reader.ts:526-527 — `fresh = now - activity.updatedAt <= 20000`; `phase = ... fresh ... ? livePhase : null`
- reader.ts:528 — `liveInfo` (liveAction/toolCount/ctx/elapsed) is only read when `phase` is truthy.

`activity.json` is written ONLY at phase transitions (`writeActivity` before each `runAgent`); there is **no heartbeat during a run** (the dev/tester `runAgent` calls at index.ts:1281/1394 don't pass `onActivity`, and `writeActivity` is never called inside the stream loop at index.ts:230-365). Real dev/tester runs measured from this repo's own transcripts last **347–628 seconds**. So for ~20s the panel shows the rich running block (spinner + elapsed + `↳ … tools … ctx`), then `fresh` flips false → glyph becomes `idle` → the task collapses to the bare `· <label> — idle` line (reader.ts:716) for the remaining 5–10 minutes, with no spinner, no elapsed, no liveAction. The founder watching a long build sees "idle" while the agent is actively working — re-creating the exact "useless" complaint this task set out to fix. The ~120ms ticker re-renders but always produces the idle line.

The test masks this with a physically-impossible fixture: `agent_start.t`=12:00:02 (reader_test.sh:194) but `activity.updatedAt`=12:01:13 (line 65/166) with `now`=12:01:14 (line 266). That makes activity 1s old (fresh) while elapsed=72s. In production `writeActivity` runs milliseconds before `agent_start`, so once elapsed reaches ~20s the activity is also ~20s stale and the running block (incl. `elapsedMs`) never renders. The `1m 12s` assertion passes only because of this artificial 71s decoupling.

`REVIEW: REQUEST-CHANGES`

BLOCKING:
- extensions/foreman/dashboard/reader.ts:526-528 — Panel liveness (spinner, role badge, `elapsedMs`, `liveAction`, tool/ctx sub-line) is gated by the 20s `activity.json` staleness, but no heartbeat updates `activity.json` during a 5–10 min `runAgent`. Result: the live block renders for only ~20s, then the active task shows as `— idle` (reader.ts:716) for the rest of the run — the elapsed counter the founder wants to watch never exceeds ~20s. Decouple panel liveness from the 20s crash-detection gate (e.g. derive freshness/elapsed from the active transcript's last-event/mtime recency, or add an activity heartbeat), so a healthy long run keeps rendering running + ticking elapsed.
- extensions/foreman/dashboard/test/reader_test.sh:65,194,266 — The RUNNING-task enrichment assertions rely on `activity.updatedAt` being 71s newer than `agent_start.t` while only 1s before `now`, which cannot occur in production and hides the staleness collapse above. Add an assertion at a realistic `now` (e.g. `agent_start.t + 60s` with `activity.updatedAt` written at the same time as `agent_start`) proving the running block + non-trivial elapsed still render mid-run.

NITS:
- extensions/foreman/index.ts:1093 — `formatStatusPanel` is always called with the default `width: 80`; the `─ FOREMAN ─…` title rule (reader.ts:683-693) won't span a wider terminal and overflows/wraps a narrower one (the task asked for a full-width title). Consider threading the real editor width if available.
- extensions/foreman/index.ts:603 + reader.ts:456-466,533 — During planning the loop writes `phase:"developer"` while `state:"planning"`, so glyph resolves to `gate` → the panel shows `◆ … needs you: plan?` even while the planner agent is actively drafting (says "needs you" when the machine is working). Relatedly, `livePhase`/`PHASE_LABEL` now accept `"planner"`/`"reviewer"` but the loop never writes those `activity.phase` values (planner→`developer`, reviewer→`tester`), so that added phase handling is only reachable via transcript-role/note inference — partly dead via the activity path.
